---
title: "Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis in Adults: A Systematic Review (2016-2026)"
subtitle: "A Systematic Literature Review"
date: "2026-03-25"
author: "Automated Literature Review Pipeline"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    cite-method: citeproc
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    linestretch: 1.5
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{prisma-flow-diagram/prisma-flow-diagram}
  docx:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
bibliography: references.bib
csl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/citation-style-language/styles/master/american-medical-association.csl
abstract: |
  **Background:** Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) is a life-threatening hyperinflammatory syndrome driven by aberrant activation of macrophages and cytotoxic T lymphocytes, leading to uncontrolled cytokine release and multi-organ dysfunction. In adults, secondary HLH predominates and carries mortality rates of 50--70%, yet remains widely under-recognized. The past decade has transformed the HLH landscape through the emergence of COVID-19-associated hyperinflammation, expanding use of chimeric antigen receptor T-cell (CAR-T) therapy, and immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI)-related toxicities, introducing novel triggers that challenge existing diagnostic and therapeutic paradigms.
  
  **Methods:** A systematic review following PRISMA guidelines searched Scopus, PubMed, and Embase for English-language peer-reviewed articles published between January 2016 and March 2026 using terms for hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis, hemophagocytic syndrome, and macrophage activation syndrome combined with adult. From 897 initial records, 783 remained after duplicate removal. Screening and full-text assessment applying a CiteScore threshold of 3.0 and excluding case reports with fewer than five patients, editorials, and pediatric-only studies yielded 50 articles across 36 journals. Quality was appraised using AMSTAR-2, the Newcastle--Ottawa Scale, QUADAS-2, and GRADE principles.
  
  **Results:** Included studies reveal advances across five domains: pathogenesis and immunological mechanisms, diagnostic criteria and biomarkers, etiological classification, treatment strategies, and HLH in the context of COVID-19. Key findings include recognition of interferon-gamma and interleukin-18 as central mediators, clinical utility of the HScore alongside HLH-2004 criteria in adults, identification of germline HAVCR2 mutations as a novel genetic predisposition, and development of targeted therapies including emapalumab and ruxolitinib. The COVID-19 pandemic generated unprecedented data on virus-associated hyperinflammation with HLH-like features, while iatrogenic HLH from CAR-T and ICI therapies emerged as a distinct clinical entity.
  
  **Conclusions:** This review synthesizes a rapidly evolving field over the past decade. Novel triggers, refined diagnostics, and emerging targeted therapies necessitate updated clinical frameworks. Knowledge gaps in adult-specific diagnostic thresholds, optimal treatment sequencing, and long-term outcomes are identified to guide future research.
---

{{< include sections/01-introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/02-methods.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/03-pathogenesis.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/04-diagnosis.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/05-etiology.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/06-treatment.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/07-covid.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/08-discussion.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/09-prisma-checklist.qmd >}}

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
